refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Derivation is the primary functional obligation in the problem domain; recording and managing are implementation concerns that belong in the DP/IM layers. The refined FR focuses on the core functional need: translating worksite mission inputs into explicit performance targets. This remains solution-neutral and avoids prescribing 'method,' 'document,' or 'history' which are design choices.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Demonstrate that given a worksite mission context (e.g., site layout, production schedule, safety constraints), the system produces a traceable set of performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that satisfy the mission objectives. Verify traceability from worksite inputs to derived targets.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall accept worksite mission context inputs (e.g., site layout, production schedule, safety policies, resource constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall derive quantitative performance targets (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that satisfy the worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall establish traceability between worksite mission inputs and derived performance targets.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance targets and their baseline values.
CR-NGHE-005.5: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance target updates and calibration changes.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement a mission-context capture module that accepts worksite inputs (safety plan, operational policies, environmental conditions) and applies a deterministic mapping function to derive performance targets; maintain an immutable audit log of all baseline versions and calibration updates with timestamps and change rationale.

DP-B: Establish a parameterized performance-target template library indexed by worksite classification (hazard level, operational mode, equipment type); allow runtime selection and instantiation of templates based on worksite context inputs, with version control and traceability metadata.

DP-C: Deploy a rule-based inference engine that accepts worksite mission context as facts and applies a curated set of domain rules (derived from safety standards and operational best practices) to synthesize performance targets; log all rule firings and intermediate conclusions for auditability.

DP-D: Create a federated data model where worksite inputs (safety plan, policies) are stored in a canonical repository; performance targets are computed on-demand via a stateless transformation service that references this repository; maintain a time-series history of all computed baselines with full lineage.

DP-E: Implement a hybrid approach combining template selection (DP-B) with rule-based refinement (DP-C); worksite context first selects a baseline template, then domain rules adjust targets based on specific operational constraints; all decisions and adjustments are logged with justification.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-E: Hybrid template-selection + rule-based refinement with full decision logging and lineage tracking

potential_sources: Template library design must remain decoupled from rule engine implementation to allow independent evolution of domain knowledge.
Worksite input schema must be stable and version-controlled to prevent cascading changes to both template selection and rule evaluation logic.
History/audit log storage must be independent of the computation path to avoid tight coupling between derivation logic and persistence.
Rule set maintenance and template curation should be owned by separate teams or processes to prevent accidental coupling of safety-critical knowledge.
coupling_check.notes: DP-E avoids monolithic coupling by separating concerns: (1) template selection is a lookup operation, (2) rule refinement is a separate inference step, (3) logging is orthogonal. Each can be modified independently if interfaces remain stable. Risk: if rule engine and template library share state or decision context without clear boundaries, coupling will emerge.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context inputs (safety plan, operational policies) are available in a structured, machine-readable format at system initialization and update events. RISK: If inputs are unstructured or inconsistently formatted, the mapping function will fail or produce unreliable targets.

ASSUMPTION: Domain rules and template baselines are maintained by subject-matter experts with authority to validate correctness against safety standards. RISK: Incorrect or outdated rules/templates will propagate to all derived performance targets; governance and review processes are critical.

ASSUMPTION: 'Documented set of performance targets' implies a formal specification artifact (e.g., JSON schema, structured table) that can be versioned and traced. RISK: If documentation is informal or manual, traceability will be lost and acceptance criteria cannot be verified.

ASSUMPTION: 'History of baselines/calibration updates' implies a time-series database or immutable log with sufficient retention and query capability. RISK: If history is not queryable or is pruned prematurely, regulatory/audit requirements may not be met.

ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are stable during a performance-target derivation cycle. RISK: If policies change mid-cycle, targets may become inconsistent; change-management process must be defined.
RISK: Rule-based refinement (DP-E) introduces interpretability burden; if rules are complex or conflicting, derived targets may be difficult to justify to auditors or operators.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: FR-NGHE-005 (refined): The NGHE solution shall derive a documented, version-controlled set of performance targets from worksite mission context (safety plan, operational policies, environmental constraints) using a hybrid approach: (1) select a baseline template matched to worksite classification, (2) apply domain rules to refine targets based on specific operational constraints, (3) log all derivation decisions with full lineage and justification, (4) maintain an immutable audit trail of all baseline versions and calibration updates with timestamps, change rationale, and traceability to source inputs. Acceptance criteria: (a) Performance targets are formally specified and machine-readable, (b) every target is traceable to at least one worksite input and one derivation rule/template, (c) audit log is queryable and supports regulatory review, (d) template and rule libraries are version-controlled and reviewed by authorized domain experts.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized decision-making engine responsible for template selection, rule-based refinement, decision logging, and lineage tracking across all operational contexts

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission/task parameters and context that drive template selection and refinement rules

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Ingests vehicle state, environmental data, and operational metrics to inform rule-based refinement decisions

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Outputs safety-critical decisions and constraints derived from rule evaluation with full lineage

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Transmits decision logs, lineage records, and refined parameters to remote operators and archives; receives feedback for iterative refinement

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: NGHE is the logical home for decision logic, template management, and rule engines. Decision logging and lineage tracking are core to intelligent guidance. Minor diffusion: SafetyAlert interface requires coordination with Safety Services for constraint validation, but decision generation remains contained.

Poor fit: Safety Services should enforce safety constraints, not generate hybrid decisions. Template selection and rule refinement are guidance functions, not safety validation. High diffusion: decision logging and lineage would leak into NGHE; rule-based refinement logic would be split across modules.

Very poor fit: Vehicle module is for vehicle control and state management, not decision-making logic. Template selection, rule refinement, and decision logging are guidance-layer concerns. Severe diffusion: all decision logic would be misplaced; telemetry consumption and tasking interpretation belong in NGHE.

Poor fit: Teleoperation handles remote operator interaction and command relay, not autonomous decision generation. Template selection and rule refinement are autonomous guidance functions. High diffusion: decision logging and lineage would need to be shared with NGHE; operator feedback loops would complicate module boundaries.

assigned_im.ownership_candidate: NGHE Solution (Next-Generation Hybrid Engine or equivalent autonomous guidance subsystem)
assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: DP-E (hybrid template-selection + rule-based refinement with full decision logging and lineage tracking) is fundamentally a decision-generation and reasoning function that belongs in the autonomous guidance layer. NGHE Solution is the natural owner because it already encapsulates mission planning, parameter optimization, and intelligent decision-making. Template selection is a guidance pattern-matching problem; rule-based refinement is a constraint-satisfaction and optimization problem; decision logging and lineage are intrinsic to explainable autonomous systems. All three components cohere around the core function of generating justified, traceable decisions.
containment_risks: Risk: If Safety Services is not tightly coupled to NGHE via SafetyAlert interface, refined decisions may bypass safety validation. Mitigation: Establish formal contract where all NGHE outputs are validated against Safety Services constraints before execution.

Risk: Lineage tracking may require access to real-time telemetry and tasking data; if these interfaces are not well-defined, NGHE may become tightly coupled to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules. Mitigation: Standardize Telemetry and Tasking interfaces as immutable event streams with versioning.

Risk: Decision logging volume may grow unbounded if all intermediate refinement steps are logged. Mitigation: Implement configurable logging levels and archival policies within NGHE; use Network Link to offload historical logs to external storage.

Risk: Lineage tracking across distributed rule engines (if rules are evaluated in parallel) may introduce race conditions or inconsistent ordering. Mitigation: Enforce sequential decision checkpoints or use distributed transaction semantics within NGHE.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. Acceptance criteria require documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained history of baselines/calibration updates. NGHE Solution's decision logging and lineage tracking directly satisfy traceability requirement. Verification can be performed by: (1) auditing decision logs for complete chain-of-custody from Tasking input through template selection to refined parameters; (2) validating that all performance targets reference source worksite context; (3) confirming baseline/calibration history is persisted and retrievable via Network Link interface.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: CLEAR. FR-CR-NGHE-005 is unambiguously allocated to NGHE Solution as the autonomous guidance layer responsible for mission-context-driven performance derivation. Worksite safety plan and operational policies flow in via Tasking interface; telemetry provides operational context via Telemetry interface. Output (SafetyAlert) carries derived constraints with full lineage. No ambiguity regarding which module owns the derivation logic or where traceability artifacts are stored.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-E (hybrid template-selection + rule-based refinement with full decision logging and lineage tracking) directly addresses FR-CR-NGHE-005. Template selection maps mission context to performance patterns; rule-based refinement adapts patterns to worksite constraints; decision logging and lineage tracking ensure traceability to worksite inputs. The DP is sufficiently detailed to guide implementation and measurable against acceptance criteria (documented targets, traceable history). No logical gap between functional requirement and design parameter.

diffusion_risks: MODERATE: SafetyAlert interface creates a dependency on Safety Services for constraint validation. If Safety Services does not maintain a formal contract for accepting NGHE-derived constraints and returning validated/rejected decisions, refined parameters may bypass safety checks. Mitigation: Establish explicit handshake protocol where SafetyAlert outputs are acknowledged and validated by Safety Services before execution; log all safety validation decisions within NGHE for lineage completeness.

MODERATE: Lineage tracking requires real-time access to Tasking and Telemetry streams. If these interfaces are not versioned or immutable, NGHE may become tightly coupled to upstream modules' internal data structures. Mitigation: Standardize Tasking and Telemetry as event-based interfaces with schema versioning; NGHE consumes only published events, not internal state.

LOW-MODERATE: Decision logging volume and distributed rule evaluation (if rules are parallelized) may introduce consistency or ordering issues. If logging is not atomic or rule evaluation order is non-deterministic, lineage may become incomplete or contradictory. Mitigation: Enforce sequential decision checkpoints within NGHE; use distributed transaction semantics or event sourcing to guarantee consistent ordering of refinement steps.

LOW: Network Link interface for offloading logs and receiving operator feedback could create a feedback loop that re-enters NGHE, complicating module boundaries. Mitigation: Clearly separate inbound feedback (used for iterative refinement) from outbound logs (immutable records); implement feedback validation to prevent circular dependencies.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: EXCELLENT CONTAINMENT (0.92 score justified). NGHE Solution is the natural and correct owner of DP-E. All three sub-components (template selection, rule-based refinement, decision logging/lineage) are cohesive decision-generation and reasoning functions that belong in the autonomous guidance layer. Template selection is a pattern-matching problem inherent to mission planning; rule-based refinement is constraint-satisfaction and optimization, core to intelligent guidance; decision logging and lineage are intrinsic to explainable autonomous systems. No logical reason to scatter these across Safety Services, Vehicle, or Teleoperation modules. Minor diffusion risk (0.08) is acceptable and well-mitigated by formal SafetyAlert interface contract and standardized input streams.

recommended_revisions: REVISION 1: Formalize SafetyAlert interface contract. Define explicit protocol: (a) NGHE outputs refined parameters with decision rationale and lineage; (b) Safety Services validates against worksite safety plan and operational policies; (c) Safety Services returns accept/reject decision with constraint violations (if any); (d) NGHE logs all safety validation outcomes in lineage. This prevents refined parameters from bypassing safety checks and ensures traceability.

REVISION 2: Standardize Tasking and Telemetry interfaces as immutable event streams with schema versioning. Define: (a) event schema (mission context, vehicle state, environmental data); (b) versioning strategy (backward compatibility rules); (c) timestamp and sequence guarantees. This decouples NGHE from upstream module internals and enables consistent lineage tracking.

REVISION 3: Implement configurable logging levels and archival policies within NGHE. Define: (a) log levels (e.g., DECISION, REFINEMENT_STEP, CONSTRAINT_CHECK); (b) retention policies (e.g., keep full logs for 30 days, summaries for 1 year); (c) archival trigger (e.g., offload to external storage via Network Link when local storage exceeds threshold). This prevents unbounded log growth while maintaining traceability.

REVISION 4: Enforce sequential decision checkpoints or distributed transaction semantics for rule evaluation. If rules are parallelized, use: (a) event sourcing to record all rule evaluations in order; (b) distributed transaction log to guarantee consistency; (c) checkpoint mechanism to ensure lineage is complete before SafetyAlert output. This prevents race conditions and ensures lineage integrity.

REVISION 5: Add explicit acceptance criterion: 'All performance targets derived by NGHE shall include lineage metadata (source worksite inputs, template selected, rules applied, safety constraints validated) queryable via Network Link interface.' This operationalizes the traceability requirement and enables verification of FR-CR-NGHE-005 compliance.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-IM, FR-DP, DP-IM) pass with high confidence, and containment is excellent (0.92). However, the gate results surface five concrete, actionable revisions that must be incorporated into the current FR-DP-IM specification before proceeding to decomposition. These revisions are not optional refinements; they are essential to operationalize traceability, prevent safety bypass, and ensure lineage integrity—all critical for a high-safety-criticality autonomous guidance function. Specifically: (1) SafetyAlert interface contract formalization is mandatory to prevent refined parameters from circumventing safety validation; (2) Tasking/Telemetry standardization as immutable event streams is necessary to decouple NGHE from upstream internals and enable consistent lineage; (3) logging level and archival policies must be defined to prevent unbounded growth while maintaining traceability; (4) sequential decision checkpoints or distributed transaction semantics are required to guarantee lineage consistency under parallelized rule evaluation; (5) explicit acceptance criterion for lineage metadata queryability operationalizes the traceability requirement and enables verification. Incorporating these revisions strengthens the DP-IM specification and reduces diffusion risk from MODERATE to LOW. Once revisions are integrated, the FR-DP-IM specification will be sufficiently detailed and risk-mitigated to support confident decomposition of sub-FRs.

revise.why: DP-E (hybrid template-selection + rule-based refinement with decision logging and lineage tracking) is logically sound and correctly allocated to NGHE Solution. However, the DP specification lacks critical implementation-facing details that the gate analysis identified as necessary to prevent safety bypass, ensure lineage integrity, and operationalize traceability. Specifically: (1) SafetyAlert interface contract must be formalized to define the handshake protocol between NGHE and Safety Services, including validation acknowledgment and constraint violation logging; (2) Tasking and Telemetry interface specifications must be upgraded to immutable event-based streams with schema versioning and timestamp/sequence guarantees, decoupling NGHE from upstream internals; (3) decision logging architecture must include configurable log levels, retention policies, and archival triggers to prevent unbounded growth; (4) rule evaluation semantics must enforce sequential checkpoints or distributed transaction guarantees to prevent race conditions and ensure lineage ordering; (5) acceptance criteria must be extended to include explicit lineage metadata queryability via Network Link. These revisions do not change the core DP logic but make it implementable, verifiable, and safe for high-criticality autonomous guidance. Revise DP-E to incorporate all five recommended revisions before proceeding to sub-FR decomposition.

assumptions: Safety Services will implement a formal contract for accepting and validating NGHE-derived constraints; if not, SafetyAlert interface must be redesigned to include inline validation logic within NGHE.

Tasking and Telemetry interfaces can be standardized as immutable event streams with schema versioning; if upstream modules cannot support event-based consumption, NGHE must implement a translation layer to decouple from internal state.
Distributed rule evaluation (if parallelized) can be made consistent via event sourcing or distributed transaction semantics; if not feasible, rule evaluation must be strictly sequential.

Network Link interface supports bidirectional communication (outbound logs, inbound feedback) without creating circular dependencies; if feedback loops are unavoidable, explicit validation and separation of concerns must be enforced.
Logging volume and archival policies are acceptable within NGHE's computational and storage budget; if not, logging must be sampled or summarized at the cost of reduced lineage granularity.
open_risks: MODERATE: SafetyAlert interface contract may not be formalized by Safety Services, leading to refined parameters bypassing safety validation. Mitigation: Establish explicit handshake protocol and log all safety validation decisions within NGHE for lineage completeness.

MODERATE: Tasking and Telemetry interfaces may not be versioned or immutable, creating tight coupling between NGHE and upstream modules. Mitigation: Standardize as event-based interfaces with schema versioning; NGHE consumes only published events.

LOW-MODERATE: Decision logging volume and distributed rule evaluation may introduce consistency or ordering issues. Mitigation: Enforce sequential decision checkpoints or use event sourcing to guarantee consistent ordering.
LOW: Network Link feedback loop could complicate module boundaries and create circular dependencies. Mitigation: Clearly separate inbound feedback from outbound logs; implement feedback validation.

SAFETY-CRITICAL: If lineage metadata is incomplete or inconsistent, traceability of performance targets to worksite inputs may be compromised, violating FR-CR-NGHE-005 acceptance criteria. Mitigation: Implement atomic decision logging and distributed transaction semantics; validate lineage completeness before SafetyAlert output.
evidence_needed: Formalized SafetyAlert interface contract document specifying: (a) NGHE output format (refined parameters, decision rationale, lineage); (b) Safety Services validation protocol (accept/reject, constraint violations); (c) logging of safety validation outcomes in NGHE lineage.

Tasking and Telemetry interface specifications as immutable event streams, including: (a) event schema (mission context, vehicle state, environmental data); (b) versioning strategy (backward compatibility rules); (c) timestamp and sequence guarantees.

NGHE logging architecture specification defining: (a) log levels (DECISION, REFINEMENT_STEP, CONSTRAINT_CHECK); (b) retention policies (e.g., 30 days full logs, 1 year summaries); (c) archival triggers and offload mechanism via Network Link.

Rule evaluation semantics specification defining: (a) sequential checkpoint mechanism or distributed transaction log; (b) event sourcing strategy (if parallelized); (c) consistency guarantees and lineage completeness validation before SafetyAlert output.

Updated acceptance criterion: 'All performance targets derived by NGHE shall include lineage metadata (source worksite inputs, template selected, rules applied, safety constraints validated) queryable via Network Link interface.' with verification method.

Proof-of-concept or prototype demonstrating: (a) end-to-end lineage traceability from Tasking input through template selection and rule refinement to SafetyAlert output; (b) consistency of lineage under parallelized rule evaluation; (c) queryability of lineage metadata via Network Link.
